RequirementText: The carpooling platform shall maintain a minimum uptime of 99.9% measured on a rolling 30-day window excluding scheduled maintenance windows communicated at least 48 hours in advance.
All authentication tokens issued by the restaurant delivery API shall be signed using AES-256 encryption and validated on every request through an OAuth 2.0 bearer token flow.
The automated data pipeline shall comply with GDPR Article 17 right-to-erasure obligations by purging all personal data within 72 hours of a verified deletion request.

The mobile driver application for the carpooling service shall render consistently on screens ranging from 4.7 inches to 12.9 inches and support both portrait and landscape orientations without layout breakage.
The restaurant delivery platform shall process a customer checkout request and return an order confirmation within 1.2 seconds at the 95th percentile under a load of 500 concurrent users.
The backend data pipeline shall be deployable as a Dockerized container image and shall run without modification on AWS ECS, Google Cloud Run, and Azure Container Instances.
The carpooling platform shall support horizontal scaling to accommodate a 10x surge in ride-matching requests during peak commuting hours without degradation of response times.
The system shall implement role-based access control (RBAC) so that restaurant managers can only view reports scoped to their own outlets and cannot access data belonging to other tenants.

The customer-facing ordering web application shall achieve a Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 Level AA conformance rating so that users with visual impairments can complete an order without assistance.

The data pipeline orchestration service shall record structured audit logs for every DAG run including start time end time exit code and operator-level error messages to facilitate post-incident analysis.

The carpooling service shall remain available for ride booking and tracking operations during partial backend failures by serving cached route data and queuing booking requests locally for up to 5 minutes.
The restaurant delivery platform shall be available to end customers 24 hours a day 7 days a week including public holidays with a maximum tolerated unplanned downtime of 4 hours per calendar month.

The data pipeline shall comply with Pakistan's Personal Data Protection Bill requirements by ensuring all personally identifiable information is stored within data centers physically located within national borders to satisfy data sovereignty obligations.

The iOS and Android driver applications shall share a single React Native codebase and be deployable to both platforms from a single CI/CD pipeline without platform-specific branching in the business logic layer.
The onboarding flow for new restaurant partners shall be completable within 10 minutes by a user with no prior training with no step requiring more than 3 user interactions to advance.

The carpooling platform's source code shall adhere to modular monorepo conventions using clearly defined package boundaries so that a developer unfamiliar with the codebase can locate and patch a fare-calculation bug within 2 hours.

The automated data pipeline shall degrade gracefully when an upstream data source becomes unreachable by skipping the affected partition logging a structured warning and continuing to process all remaining partitions without operator intervention.
The web-based restaurant delivery dashboard shall match the brand color palette defined in the corporate design system using hex codes #E63946 for primary actions and #F1FAEE for background surfaces.
The carpooling platform shall execute a ride-match algorithm and return a ranked list of available drivers to the requesting passenger within 800 milliseconds end-to-end at median load.
The restaurant delivery application server shall be hardware-agnostic and shall operate correctly on ARM64 and x86-64 processor architectures without recompilation or configuration changes.

The carpooling platform database cluster shall scale write throughput automatically from 500 to 50000 transactions per second by adding read replicas and sharding the rides table by geographic region without manual DBA intervention.

All payment card data transmitted through the restaurant delivery checkout flow shall be protected using TLS 1.3 and the platform shall undergo a third-party penetration test at least once per calendar year with all critical findings remediated within 30 days.

The data pipeline platform shall maintain SLA availability of 99.5% for all scheduled pipeline executions measured monthly with automated alerting triggered when cumulative downtime exceeds 3 hours in any rolling 30-day period.

The restaurant delivery platform shall comply with applicable consumer protection regulations in each operating jurisdiction and shall present legally required allergen disclosures prominently on every menu item detail page prior to any purchase action.

The carpooling application shall display a consistent visual hierarchy across all screens using the Inter typeface at designated size scales and a maximum of 3 accent colors to ensure a professional and coherent look and feel.

The data pipeline framework shall be instrumented so that a data engineer can add a new transformation step by creating a single Python module conforming to the operator interface without modifying any existing pipeline definition files.
The automated ETL pipeline shall operate within the resource limits of a standard 4-vCPU 16 GB RAM Kubernetes pod and shall not require elevated cluster-admin privileges to run scheduled jobs.

The carpooling platform shall return live vehicle location updates to the passenger application at a refresh interval not exceeding 3 seconds under normal network conditions and no greater than 10 seconds under degraded 3G connectivity.

The restaurant delivery web application shall be deployable as a cross-platform Progressive Web App installable on Windows macOS Linux iOS and Android without publishing separate native binaries to platform-specific app stores.

The carpooling platform's microservices architecture shall allow the ride-matching service to be scaled independently to 200 instances during sporting events or public holidays without requiring redeployment of the payment or notification services.

The carpooling application shall enforce multi-factor authentication for all driver accounts and shall store credential hashes using bcrypt with a minimum cost factor of 12 with no plaintext or reversibly encrypted passwords permitted in the database.

New users of the restaurant delivery application shall be able to browse menus add items to a cart and place a first order within 4 minutes of opening the application for the first time with no prior product training.

The automated data pipeline shall emit OpenTelemetry-compliant trace spans for every task execution enabling full distributed tracing from ingestion source to data warehouse sink without requiring custom instrumentation in individual operator code.

In the event of a primary database node failure the carpooling platform shall automatically promote a hot standby replica and resume accepting ride bookings within 30 seconds using a pre-configured failover policy with no manual operator intervention required.

The restaurant delivery platform shall sustain a monthly availability rate of no less than 99.95% for the order placement and tracking APIs with planned maintenance excluded and unplanned outages compensated through customer credit in line with the published SLA.

The carpooling platform shall process all driver and passenger personal data in compliance with applicable data protection law and shall obtain explicit opt-in consent before sharing location history with third-party analytics providers as required under data sovereignty frameworks.

All public-facing screens of the restaurant delivery mobile application shall use a minimum touch target size of 44x44 points consistent spacing tokens from the design system and legible font sizes no smaller than 14pt to meet brand look and feel standards.

The data pipeline codebase shall maintain a test coverage rate of at least 85% across all transformation modules and any new operator merged to the main branch shall include unit tests and a documented rollback procedure.
The carpooling platform shall operate fully within a standard corporate network environment including behind an HTTP forward proxy without requiring firewall rule exceptions beyond ports 80 and 443.

The restaurant delivery platform shall generate a complete order confirmation page and send a push notification to the customer within 2 seconds of payment authorization at the 99th percentile under peak load of 2000 concurrent checkouts.

The carpooling driver application shall be packaged as a cross-platform Dockerized build artifact so that the QA team can spin up a fully functional local environment on any developer machine regardless of host operating system within 5 minutes.

The restaurant delivery platform's order management service shall scale elastically to handle a 20x increase in order volume during promotional flash-sale events by auto-provisioning additional compute nodes through the cloud provider's managed autoscaling group.

The carpooling platform shall conduct automated static analysis of all API endpoints using OWASP ZAP as part of every CI pipeline run and shall reject any merge that introduces a high-severity vulnerability as defined by the OWASP Top 10.

A first-time passenger on the carpooling platform shall be able to understand the ride booking flow and complete their first booking request without consulting documentation with at least 90% task-completion rate observed in usability testing sessions.

The automated data pipeline shall publish a data quality scorecard to the internal metrics dashboard after every pipeline run reporting completeness consistency and timeliness dimensions to enable SRE teams to monitor pipeline health without querying raw logs.

The data pipeline's message queue layer shall implement redundant nodes across two availability zones and shall continue processing events at full throughput if one availability zone becomes unreachable using an active-active failover configuration.

The carpooling platform's core ride-matching and booking services shall maintain 99.8% availability with automatic health checks every 60 seconds and self-healing pod restarts so that passengers experience no service interruption during routine infrastructure maintenance.

The restaurant delivery platform shall undergo annual compliance audits against PCI-DSS Level 1 requirements and all audit findings classified as critical shall be remediated and re-verified within 14 calendar days of the audit report delivery.

The restaurant delivery partner portal shall present a clean visual interface adopting a card-based layout with consistent 8-pixel grid spacing neutral background tones and a single primary brand color used exclusively for call-to-action elements.

The carpooling backend services shall be structured so that the fare engine can be updated and redeployed independently without requiring changes to the matching service or the notification service maintaining zero-downtime deployments via blue-green switching.

The automated data pipeline shall be operable by a data analyst with no Linux system administration experience using a self-service web UI that exposes pipeline scheduling backfill triggering and log inspection without requiring SSH access to production servers.
The carpooling platform's map rendering service shall deliver an initial tile load for a 15-kilometer radius around the user's location within 1.5 seconds on a standard 4G mobile connection.

The restaurant delivery platform's microservice containers shall be hardware-agnostic and the same container image shall execute identically in local developer Docker Desktop environments and in the production Kubernetes cluster without environment-specific code paths.

The carpooling platform's notification service shall be independently scalable and shall handle up to 1 million push notification dispatches per hour by consuming from a partitioned Kafka topic without impacting the latency of the ride-matching service.

The carpooling platform shall protect all driver identity documents stored in the system using field-level AES-256 encryption and shall restrict access to those documents through RBAC policies granting read permission only to the compliance officer role.

Customers of the restaurant delivery application shall be able to customise an order including selecting toppings specifying dietary exclusions and adding delivery instructions using no more than 3 screen transitions with all primary actions reachable within one thumb's reach on a standard smartphone.

The data pipeline platform shall emit standardised operational metrics including job queue depth average task duration and late-arrival record counts to a Prometheus endpoint so that platform KPIs can be visualised in Grafana dashboards without custom exporters.

The restaurant delivery platform shall implement circuit-breaker patterns on all third-party payment gateway integrations so that a gateway timeout of more than 3 seconds triggers graceful degradation by surfacing an alternative payment method to the customer rather than presenting an error page.

The carpooling service driver-facing application shall be available offline for viewing upcoming bookings and accessing navigation instructions for the next scheduled ride using locally cached data when the device loses internet connectivity.

The automated data pipeline platform shall retain complete lineage metadata for every dataset transformation and shall provide a queryable API so that data governance teams can trace any field in a report back to its raw source record to satisfy compliance audit requirements.
